assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been declared and recognised as a foreign-currency monetary receivable/payable before elimination on consolidation.
The question is limited to hedge accounting classification at the consolidated financial statement level under IFRS.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies recognised assets and liabilities as eligible hedged item families, and the foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item is specifically addressed for consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats forecast transactions as a separate top-level hedged item family, including certain intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 establish a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation.
